title: oxypolis rigidior subsp. longifolia
intro: Oxypolis rigidior subsp. longifolia, also known as sweet false parsley, is a perennial herb native to New Zealand and Australia, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Oxypolis rigidior subsp. longifolia include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, ease menstrual cramps, and support respiratory health. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots are also used in herbal remedies, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
